mainEntity.name: Common Industrial Workplace Accident Injury Questions — St. George
mainEntity.text: Common Industrial Workplace Accident Injury Questions — St. George

acceptedAnswer.text:
Clients in St. George frequently ask these questions about Industrial Workplace Accident Injury. Davis & Sanchez (Just Work Comp Law) provides straightforward answers based on years of experience.

Q: Can I be fired for filing a workers compensation claim?
A: No. It is illegal for an employer to terminate, demote, or retaliate against an employee for filing a workers compensation claim. If you experience retaliation after filing a claim, you may have additional legal remedies including a wrongful termination lawsuit. Document any changes in your work conditions after filing your claim.

Q: How much does workers compensation pay?
A: Workers compensation temporary disability benefits typically pay 60-67% of your average weekly wage, subject to state maximum limits. Permanent disability benefits vary based on the severity of your impairment, your age, occupation, and state-specific formulas. Total compensation can range from a few thousand dollars for minor injuries to hundreds of thousands for severe, permanent disabilities.

Q: What if my workers compensation claim is denied?
A: A denied workers compensation claim can be appealed. Common denial reasons include missed deadlines, disputes about whether the injury is work-related, or insufficient medical documentation. An attorney can file an appeal, gather additional evidence, and represent you at hearings before a workers compensation judge.

Have more questions? Call Davis & Sanchez (Just Work Comp Law) at 801-746-0290 for a personalized consultation.
